
# Polishing {.unnumbered}

![](images/wrangling.png){width="650" fig-align="center"} 

<p font-size=55pt align="center">
**Cleaned data may require additional polishing.**
</p>

This might involve combining datasets from multiple sources, transforming, and imputing or removing missing values.

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: false

import pandas as pd
```

## Combining

Dataframes can be combined along the vertical axis using ```concat()```.

![](images/concat.png){width="450" fig-align="center"} 

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
```{python}
#| echo: True
df1 = pd.DataFrame({'A': ['purple', 'red', 'orange'], 
                    'B': [1, 2, 3], 
                    'C': ['C0', 'C1', 'C2'],
                    'D': ['dinosaur', 'drain', 'draw']})
df1
```
:::
::: {.column}
```{python}
#| echo: True
df2 = pd.DataFrame({'A': ['green', 'blue', 'yellow'], 
                    'B': [4, 5, 6], 
                    'C': ['C0', 'C1', 'C2'],
                    'E': ['elephant', 'empty', 'easel']})
df2
```
:::
:::

::: {.columns}
::: {.column}
```{python}
#| echo: True
#| output: False
# Concatenate the two DataFrames
result = pd.concat([df1, df2])
result
```
:::
::: {.column}
```{python}
#| echo: False
# Concatenate the two DataFrames along rows
concatenated = pd.concat([df1, df2])
concatenated
```
:::
:::

Dataframes can be combined along the horizontal axis using ```merge()```.

![](images/merge.png){width="450" fig-align="center"} 

```{python}
#| echo: True
# Merge the two DataFrames along rows
merged = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='C')
merged
```

```{python}
#| echo: True
# Merge the two DataFrames along rows
merged = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='C', suffixes=['_df1', '_df2'])
merged
```

```{python}
#| echo: True
#| output: False
# Merge the two DataFrames along rows
merged = pd.merge(df1, df2, on='C', suffixes=['_df1', '_df2'])
merged
```

:::{.callout-note}
###  Learn more about the variety of join operations via the [documentation](https://pandas.pydata.org/docs/user_guide/merging.html).
:::


## Transforming

Datasets often need to be transformed between wide and long form data. 

![](images/pivot.png){width="450" fig-align="center"} 

```{python}
#| echo: False
proteins = pd.read_csv('data/example_proteins.csv')
```

Pandas provides this functionality in the form of ```pd.pivot``` for long to wide:

```{python}
#| echo: True
#| output: False
pd.pivot_table(
        proteins, 
        index='Subcellular Location', 
        columns=['Function'], 
        values='Molecular Weight (kDa)' 
              )
```

::: {.callout-note}
### The equivalent function for wide to long is ```pd.melt```.
:::


## Writing

Just as we can read a variety of data types using pandas, we can similarly save pandas dataframes.

![](images/pandas-save.png){width="500" fig-align="center"} 

```{python}
#| echo: True
# loc_counts.to_csv('data/example_grouped.csv')
```

## In practice


::: {.callout-caution}

### Exercises

3.4 Combining datasets
:::
